generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance SwingAngleEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Swing Angle Encoder measuring the crane swing angle"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SwingAngleDataTransmitted
]

instance Anemometer : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Anemometer measuring ambient wind speed"
    DTDFVocab:producedData WindSpeedDataTransmitted
]

instance MotorStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor reading motor controller states (position, speed, current)"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorStateDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction (DataTransmitted)
instance SwingAngleDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SwingAngleEncoder
]

instance WindSpeedDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom Anemometer
]

instance MotorStateDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorStateSensor
]

// C10: Data (runtime time‑series)
instance SwingAngleData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time‑series of swing‑angle values"
    DTDFVocab:fromData SwingAngleDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TimescaleDBEnabler
]

instance WindSpeedData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time‑series of wind‑speed values"
    DTDFVocab:fromData WindSpeedDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TimescaleDBEnabler
]

instance MotorStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time‑series of motor positions, speeds and currents"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorStateDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TimescaleDBEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance MQTTBrokerEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Mosquitto MQTT broker for publish/subscribe signalling between services"
    DTDFVocab:enables TrajectoryGeneratorService,
                SimulationService,
                ContinuousValidationService,
                DashboardService,
                DataLoggingService
]

instance TimescaleDBEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "TimescaleDB time‑series database storing logged, simulated and validation data"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataLoggingService,
                DashboardService,
                SimulationService,
                ContinuousValidationService
]

instance KinematicModelEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler exposing the crane kinematic model to trajectory generation and simulation"
    DTDFVocab:enables TrajectoryGeneratorService,
                SimulationService
]

// C10: Models
instance KinematicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Kinematic model of the gantry crane used for trajectory generation and physics simulation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo KinematicModelEnabler
]

instance CADModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "CAD geometry of the gantry crane structure"
    // CAD model is stored for reference; no specific enabler needed
]

// C6: Services
instance TrajectoryGeneratorService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Computes optimal swing‑free trajectories using the kinematic model"
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimalTrajectoryInsight,
                IssueTrajectoryAction
]

instance SimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Runs real‑time physics‑based simulation of the crane and its controllers"
    DTDFVocab:provides SimulatedTrajectoryInsight,
                RunSimulationAction
]

instance ContinuousValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuously compares logged measurements with simulated trajectories and raises alerts"
    DTDFVocab:provides ValidationMetricInsight,
                NotifyOperatorAction
]

instance DashboardService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Grafana dashboard visualising historic and live crane data"
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualAlertInsight,
                DisplayDashboardAction
]

instance DataLoggingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Logs all crane state data into TimescaleDB"
    DTDFVocab:provides PersistDataAction
]

// C17: Insights
instance OptimalTrajectoryInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimal motion plan guaranteeing zero residual swing"
]

instance SimulatedTrajectoryInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted crane trajectory generated by the physics simulation"
]

instance ValidationMetricInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Distance‑metric score comparing measured and simulated trajectories"
]

instance VisualAlertInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Grafana visual alert when validation metric exceeds the configured threshold"
]

// C17: Actions
instance IssueTrajectoryAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Publish optimal trajectory to the crane controller via MQTT"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance RunSimulationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Execute a given trajectory within the real‑time simulation"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance NotifyOperatorAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Send validation breach notification to the operator through Grafana"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DisplayDashboardAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Render live and historical data on the Grafana UI"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance PersistDataAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Insert recorded crane state into TimescaleDB"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The Control Application sends movement commands to the Physical Crane Interface, which translates them into controller signals for the cart and hoist motors (Stepper motors driven by TMC4671‑Eval controllers). Commands originate from higher‑level services such as the Trajectory Generator Service (optimal trajectories) and are issued as Python API calls to the interface. Feedback from the crane (position, swing angle) is provided back to the Logging Application and Continuous Validation Service. The documents do not detail command message formats, authentication, or explicit validation before actuation."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The digital‑twin follows a container‑based orchestration pattern modelled with the C4 approach. Six Docker containers provide the twin’s core functions: a TimescaleDB database, a Grafana dashboard, a Trajectory Generator service (kinematics model), a Simulation service, a Continuous Validation service, and an MQTT broker for signalling between services. All containers are launched together with a single Docker‑Compose command, establishing a shared network. Python applications (Physical Crane Interface, Control Application, Logging Application, Human‑Machine Interface) run in separate containers or on the host, interacting with the crane controllers and the MQTT broker. Resource management relies on Docker container isolation, while persistent time‑series data is stored in TimescaleDB and visualised through Grafana."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model fidelity is achieved by using a kinematic model that generates trajectories without residual swing, and a simulation model that reproduces the crane dynamics. Validation is performed continuously by the Continuous Validation Service, which compares logged sensor measurements of executed trajectories with the simulated trajectories, computes distance metrics, and checks them against a predefined threshold. If the threshold is exceeded, the operator is notified through the Grafana dashboard. The approach reuses model validation techniques described in prior publications and is further extended with time‑series classification for fault detection, providing quantitative assurance of model correctness."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "All software components run on a single personal computer. Docker containers host the TimescaleDB database, the Eclipse Mosquitto MQTT broker and the Grafana dashboard; they communicate over the host’s network via MQTT and PostgreSQL connections. The Python services (Control Application, Logging Application, Simulation Service, etc.) run directly on the host and interact with the containers locally. No specific bandwidth, latency, redundancy or security protocol specifications are provided."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The exemplar is deployed on‑premise on a personal computer. Docker Compose is used to start the TimescaleDB, MQTT broker and Grafana containers automatically; the Python modules are installed with pip (gantrylib). Storage is provided by the TimescaleDB container; computational resources are those of the host PC. The deployment model is a single‑node Docker‑compose setup; scalability beyond this node is not discussed. Availability is supported by Docker Compose’s start‑up scripts that initialize the database and dashboard on first run."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Integration with other components uses standard open‑source protocols. Inter‑service communication is performed via MQTT, and data persistence/visualisation rely on TimescaleDB and Grafana accessed through their native APIs. The Python programs communicate with the physical crane’s controllers via a custom interface (not further specified). No external enterprise systems, OPC‑UA, REST, or other interoperability standards are mentioned in the documents."
]
